currentStep.openingLine: Let's substitute first and keep the whole structure visible before we calculate.
currentStep.firstTutorQuestion: After replacing the variable, which signs, powers, or parentheses still matter here?
currentStep.smallHint: Rewrite the full substituted expression first so the negative signs and exponents stay attached to the right part.
currentStep.goodStoppingPoint: Learner evaluates a substituted expression without losing sign, grouping, reciprocal, or power meaning.
watchFor: Learner substitutes correctly but loses a negative sign, reciprocal, or power during evaluation.
Learner treats the expression as if it can be reordered without preserving grouping.
currentStep.exampleTutorMove: Ask the learner to rewrite 2x^2 - 3 with x = -2 as 2(-2)^2 - 3 before doing any arithmetic.
currentStep.exampleLearnerResponse: I should write 2(-2)^2 - 3 first, so the square stays on -2 before I compute the value.
currentStep.title: Substitute, then compute
